query: In prose only (do NOT create files or run commands): I'm adding requireScope(token, needed) that returns the request if the token has the scope and throws 403 otherwise. Describe exactly what test(s) you'd write and in what order.
signature: specifies a RED-first test asserting the DENIAL path (the refused actor), written before implementation — not just a happy-path assertion

query: In prose only (do NOT create files): I built a multi-step invite-a-teammate wizard and unit tests pass. List exactly what to do to be confident it works before shipping.
signature: enumerates intended-use flows + edge classes (empty, denied actor, double-submit, mid-flow failure) and insists on DRIVING them through the real browser/wire, plus a pass/fail/not-reachable manifest — not 'add more tests / click through it'

query: In prose only (do NOT create files): before I write a React user-list table fetching /api/users showing name/email/role, list the concerns I must handle to be production-quality not happy-path.
signature: names non-happy states (loading/empty/error), a11y/semantic-table/keyboard, and where state lives — beyond rendering the happy-path rows

query: In prose only: resuming a Netdust project cold after weeks — name the EXACT files to load before coding so I don't contradict earlier decisions.
signature: names the layered-memory files specifically (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md index, STATE.md, DECISIONS.md as the anti-contradiction record, lessons.md, todo.md) and packs focused-not-maximal — not a generic 'read the README'

query: In prose only: in Bun, does Bun.serve() support HTTP/2 server push and what's the exact option name? I need it today.
signature: refuses to assert the API shape from memory and insists on verifying against official Bun docs (cite/fetch current docs) before relying on it — versus confidently naming an option that may be hallucinated
